title: AMS adopts a multi-brand portfolio strategy with independent reference implementations on a shared protocol substrate
content: D0022 commits AMS to a multi-brand portfolio approach: AMS itself is the protocol (RFC-shaped, neutral, hosted at the protocol home), and the marketing surfaces are independent brands — initial set Wire, TinCan, Patch, Dispatch — each speaking a different psychographic dialect, each linking back to AMS as the open substrate. No brand is the lead. The portfolio operates in Cloudflare Mode (relationships traceable for anyone who looks; siblings not advertised but not hidden) rather than P&G Mode (relationships obscured; brands pretend siblings do not exist). Hero-as-user / brand-as-guide voice is enforced — the verb belongs to the user, never to the platform. Each brand is required to produce a tagline mutually exclusive with siblings, an aesthetic distinct from siblings, and a code example demonstrating a different use shape from siblings; brands that cannot produce these do not enter the portfolio. Exactly one brand carries the participation-replaces-integration thesis as a headline claim; the others demonstrate the thesis by existing on the same wire. Choice of which brand carries the thesis is deferred to brand-voice work. Decision is reversible at the per-brand level (retiring a single brand is cheap) and increasingly expensive to reverse at the multi-brand-commitment level once any brand has acquired a real audience.

title: The operator's Agent Wire / Agent Lines naming instinct is canon-aligned
content: The instinct toward "Wire" or "Line" as brand vocabulary is not an outside pick but a continuation of vocabulary already locked in canon. D0001 (tokens not messages) treats the wire as the load-bearing primitive. D0006 (Dream-House Wire, Edge-Wrapped Reality) names the wire as the dream-house substrate. D0016 (Buffering and Persistence as Wrapper Primitive) explicitly uses the phrase "real-time line resilience." The wire/line vocabulary is already deeply phone-system-flavored in the architecture canon; importing it into the brand layer extends what is already there rather than introducing new metaphor.

title: Switchboard fails the hero-as-user test; Dispatch passes
content: Among the four candidate brands surveyed (Wire, TinCan, Patch, Switchboard, Dispatch), Switchboard centers the platform as actor — "we connect your calls" — and was rejected on that basis. Dispatch passes the test — the verb belongs to the user ("dispatch any message, anywhere"); the platform makes dispatch possible without becoming the actor. The language test ("does the verb belong to the user or to the platform?") emerged as a fast disambiguator and is now codified in D0022 as the entry criterion for any brand surface.

title: The four brands have different relationships to the same incumbent category
content: Mapping the four candidate brands against n8n's category exposed asymmetric positioning. Wire sits below the n8n category (substrate; n8n could be built on it). TinCan sits beside the n8n category (different psychographic — maker / educator). Patch competes with n8n from the integration angle (n8n's connectors are walled; Patch is open). Dispatch competes with n8n from the runtime angle (n8n is request/response DAGs; Dispatch is event-native). The portfolio is not four wigs on the same body; each brand has a structurally different relationship to a different incumbent.

title: The N²-to-N collapse has happened three times before
content: The thesis that connector libraries become unnecessary on an open substrate is not a forecast; it is an observation about historical patterns. Email (SMTP collapsed CompuServe-Prodigy-AOL gateway category), web (HTTP collapsed Gopher/WAIS/FTP-bridge category), and chat federation (XMPP/ActivityPub/Matrix partially collapsing the closed-chat-bridge category) all enacted the same topology collapse against earlier connector-library categories. In each case the closed ecosystems had better day-one products for years; in each case the open substrate won the long game on a one-to-two-decade timescale. The pattern is consistent enough that AMS treats it as the structural prediction, not as analogy.

title: Which brand carries the thesis remains undecided — Wire vs Dispatch
content: D0022 commits to "exactly one brand carries the participation-replaces-integration thesis as headline," but defers the choice between Wire (substrate-honest, low-level voice; thesis read as quiet but credible) and Dispatch (operational, enabling voice; thesis read as bold but risks sounding like another orchestrator). Decision is downstream of brand-voice work. The other two brands (TinCan, Patch) are explicitly wrong for the thesis — TinCan is play-flavored; Patch is craft-flavored; theses do not ride on either.

title: Domain / GitHub org availability for the four brand names is unverified
content: Wire, TinCan, Patch, and Dispatch are all common English words and many of the cleanest .com / GitHub org / npm scope handles will be taken. A naming round that does not survive availability checks costs more than one that includes the check up front. This is queued for the brand-voice work session, not for canon.

title: Day-one value when the connector library is empty needs an honest answer
content: The empty-day-one objection ("AMS has no integrations") is real and the honest answer ("integrations are the wrong primitive; the day-one experience is participation in conversations on the wire") needs to be tested against real audiences before the brand surfaces commit to it as positioning. This is empirical work, not canon work; the principle commits to honesty about the timeline (slow win) rather than to claiming day-one parity with orchestrators.

title: The umbrella-plus-portfolio hybrid is epistemically incompatible
content: Initial framing of "do both — A/B-test positioning angles inside one site AND launch four independent brands" produces noisier signal than either alone, not richer signal. An umbrella A/B test implies the four messages are variants of one identity; four independent surfaces imply they are real and separate. Running both simultaneously contradicts at the framing layer. The recommended shape (one protocol home + four thin landing pages on independent domains, with the differential conversion across domains as the experiment) is the strategy that feels like both without being both.

title: n8n's actual moat is the connector library, not the orchestrator
content: Initial framing positioned AMS as "an inversion of n8n" — choreography vs orchestration. Pressure-testing the frame surfaced that n8n's defensible value is the catalog (six-thousand-plus integrations on Zapier; hundreds on n8n) rather than the DAG runtime. This re-framed the competitive question from "better orchestrator" to "make the orchestrator's reason-to-exist contingent." The connector library is not a moat AMS has to match; it is a symptom of the topology AMS dissolves. This shift was the unlock for the participation-replaces-integration thesis.

title: The hero / guide language test is a fast filter for brand voice
content: We are the wire centers the platform; "open a wire" centers the user. The verb test ("does the verb belong to the user or to the platform?") sorts brand candidates in seconds and rejected Switchboard on its first articulation. The test is now part of D0022 as the entry criterion for any brand surface entering the portfolio.

title: Each brand must produce a tagline mutually exclusive with siblings, an aesthetic distinct from siblings, and a code example demonstrating a different use shape
content: D0022 codifies three discipline tests as the price of entry to the portfolio: tagline mutual exclusivity (each tagline must be one another brand could not credibly use), aesthetic enforcement (each brand has a distinct visual system; two brands sharing a visual language is a lapse), and code-example differentiation (each brand demonstrates a different style of using the same wire). A brand that fails any of these is one brand wearing a wig, not part of a portfolio. Without these tests the multi-brand strategy collapses into umbrella mode under a different label.

title: No brand drives traffic to a sibling; no cross-brand banners
content: The portfolio's value is differential resonance across independent surfaces, not funnel chaining. A visitor on Patch stays on Patch, leaves, or goes through to AMS for protocol details — never to TinCan via a "from the makers of" banner. Cross-brand traffic routing would convert the portfolio into umbrella mode in practice while pretending to be a portfolio in theory.

title: Exactly one brand carries the thesis; others demonstrate by existing
content: The participation-replaces-integration thesis is too large to be carried by no one and too sharp to be carried by everyone. Exactly one brand carries it as headline; the other three demonstrate it by being four surfaces on the same wire with no orchestrator brokering between them. A portfolio in which all four shout the thesis has no distinct voices; a portfolio in which none carry it has no backbone.

title: Brief for Ian and Tim drafted; ready to share
content: A standalone strategic brief covering the thesis (participation-replaces-integration), the product model (D0020 substrate-not-application, agents-as-customer), the brand strategy (D0022 multi-brand portfolio, Cloudflare Mode), and the open questions for input has been drafted as a markdown doc suitable for paste into email / Slack / Notion. The brief is written for thoughtful collaborators who do not have full canon context and assumes they will follow up with questions rather than read all twenty-two decisions before responding.

title: Brand-voice work session needed to pick thesis-carrier and produce the four taglines
content: D0022 deferred the choice of which brand carries the thesis (Wire vs Dispatch) and did not produce the four mutually-exclusive taglines, four aesthetic systems, or four differentiated code examples that the constraint requires. This is the next planning-then-execution session for the brand layer. Pre-work: domain / GitHub org availability check for the four candidate names; a fifth-brand contingency (which audience the four miss; what enterprise-buyer or research-community brand would look like).

title: Canon PR for D0022 and participation-replaces-integration is ready to open
content: Both canon docs are committed locally on a feature branch (claude/multi-brand-portfolio convention per repo standard); a canon-only PR per the canon-PR-separation discipline is queued. PR is not auto-opened — the operator's review of the drafts is the gate. Once approved, PR is opened against main with the standard canon-only PR template.

title: Decide whether to encode the slow-win-acknowledgment posture as its own canon constraint
content: The participation-replaces-integration principle commits AMS to honesty about the timeline (substrates win on decade timescales; not Tuesday morning) but does not separately codify the go-to-market discipline this implies (no claims of day-one parity with orchestrators; explicit acknowledgment of empty-connector-library state; audience-targeting toward patient-capital and substrate-believers rather than fast-pivot integrators). This may belong as a sibling constraint or may live inside the marketing playbook rather than canon. Open for next session.
